sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Routine follow-up for chronic medical conditions and concerns regarding fatigue and a testicular nodule.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports fatigue, primarily at the end of the day, and difficulty maintaining his previous cycling pace (previously able to ride 40 miles, now limited to 20-30 miles). He attributes this to age and general fatigue. He also mentions a palpable nodule in his right testicle, discovered recently, and expresses concern but does not appear overly worried. He underwent an aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair on 1/22/2013. He was previously seen by Dr. Lewis for an echocardiogram in September 2019. He has not been seen at Hopkins since. He states his wife and friends have noted pursed lip breathing during exhalation. He has completed his COVID-19 vaccinations.
Findings
The patient's alkaline phosphatase level was elevated at 156. His lipid panel showed a total cholesterol of 247, HDL of 66, LDL of 166, and triglycerides at 74. His tsh was normal at 2.68. His CBC was unremarkable. His most recent vitamin D level was at the high end of normal at 94. Examination revealed pursed lip breathing and a low exhalation phase. A two to three millimeter palpable nodule was noted on the right testicle, without signs of hernia. Lung fields appear normal.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient's chronic medical conditions, including the aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair, are stable. Elevated alkaline phosphatase suggests a possible bone-related etiology, warranting further investigation. The reported fatigue and pursed lip breathing require further evaluation, including pulmonary function testing. The testicular nodule requires urological evaluation.
Plan
Order an echocardiogram and a stress test. Order an alkaline phosphatase and a complete metabolic panel. Order pulmonary function testing. Refer the patient to urology for evaluation of the right testicular nodule. Recommend a follow-up appointment with cardiology, specifically with Dr. Vincent Sanchez. Schedule a return visit in approximately one year for a wellness check, sooner if clinically indicated. The nurse will administer the MMR vaccine today and schedule the shingles vaccine for next month.
